channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Something is coming soon — 24 replies — 96.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Something is coming soon

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-04-19 20:14 UTC

OP

If you’re wondering where the recent FUD is coming from it’s because we’re only months away from the release of the CyberCode 2.0 testnet. As an insider, you have no idea what the importance of CbC 2.0 is going to be for LINK when the alpha releases around the end of November (give or take a few months) so I will spoonfeed you out of kindness of heart:

CyberCode 2.0 secures the ledger cryptographically to encode transactions at 20x of the legacy infrastructure. It achieves this by taking blockchain transactions to the BbC-2026 layer and parsing them through the node network incentivized by super linear sequencing (SLSeq) which Sergey said is very close to the finish line. This is all dynamically incentivized through semi-permeable staking (SPS) allowing LINK token holders to benefit for verifying data both on the main chain and the Blockchain Byzantine Consensus 2026 layer. No other project is even close to doing this in the oracle space rn. It’s not even close!

The obvious benefits of super fast transaction speeds (obSFTS) also brings exponential economies of scale. What this means is if farmer has 50 Kg pig for sale, he can put the beast to market on the blockchain to verify the weight in a trust less system faster than global SWIFT system. Soon any business who wants to stay relevant will have to buy LINK to have access to the CyberCode 2.0 feature. A truly unique proposition! Those who don’t use this system (powered by LINK) will get left behind. So get your ducks in a row. Just keep buying and NEVER sell. We are all in this together LINKbloodbros.

Scammer motherfuckers want you to think that banks and other organizations are not currently fulling their bags with cheap LINK as we speak. DON’T NOT LET THEM WIN! If you hold 50 or more LINK right now, you already made it. Immediately when we breach the $10 resistance it’ll be too late for you. You have been warned. So go buy many LINK for you dream to come true.

Early replies (first 3)

62125665
>>62125647
DR;NS

62125781
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless???

62125919
FUDDIES, ROLL OUT

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125940
>BBC 2026

62125959
>MFW Stinkies still think they're relevant.

62125996
>>62125647
what if someone hacks the cybercode?

better wait a few more years for the PoC testnet beta to pass its initial audit round

62126130
This is pasta, right? I remember reading something just like this a long time ago

62126215
Holy fuck a good opportunity to buy a house with gf has come up and I might seriously have to sell my measly stack before it fuckin moons, I can't believe this shit Ive been holding for fucking years and years

62126221
>see the word "insider" on a /biz/ post
>hide thread

62126836
>>62125647
Delete this

62126992
>testnet

62127837
>>62125647
>technobabble word salad

62127858
>>62125647
LINK was supposed to moon with CCIP (See See I Pee) and steaking (full version will never be released btw). This CbC2.0 bullshit sounds like that BBC(A) shit that old jewish bitch at CLL came up with. Unfortunately the kind of jews at CLL are not the ones that make you money, but rather the scamming and subverting kind. We linkies will never makeit unless Sirgay unironically stops shoving bigmacs in every hole of his constantly (not happening).

62127911
the best part of this pasta is the farmer and pig

62130613
>>62125647
>BbC-2026
Hahaha very funny

62130614
Here's the real "something that is coming soon". Payment abstraction.

62130617
New things are coming with privacy technology, AI oracles also.

62131806
>>62130614
hasnt payment abstraction been a thing for like 3 years now, stinkie?

62131808
>>62125647
Price + Chart, also
>two more copes

62131814
DR; NB

62131940
>>62125647
CCPIG

62131995
>>62131806
payment abstraction v2 right now, fuddie

62132082
>>62125647
>Sergey said is very close to the finish line
fucking lol, ok

62132691
>>62131940
if they made a sergey funko pop /biz/ would buy it

item.title: no job? Maybe you just lack the WORK ETHIC — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
no job? Maybe you just lack the WORK ETHIC

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-04-19 20:19 UTC

OP

no job? Maybe you just lack the WORK ETHIC

Early replies (first 3)

62132619
>>62132612
that salary lol. even in europe thats considered low. they really have not shame.

62132640
>>62132619
it comes with housing

62132646
>>62132612
>literal back breaking labor with genuine risk of permanent injury
>$30-36k/yr
>USA poverty line currently set at $33k

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62132656
>>62132640
that must be one hell of a housing

62132663
>>62132656
add your rent/mortgage, utilities, insurance, etc to the salary and get back to us

62132664
>>62132612
Sounds comfy i would apply if I was american.
>6000 applicants 8 month old ad

62132673
>>62132663
Earning below $33k (literal poverty line) at a McJob gets you access to section 8 (free housing), EBT (free food), Medicaid (free healthcare), and other govt benefits that all total up to more than whateverthis posting is offering. There is literally zero benefit to this trash job.

62132699
>>62132656
>at must be one hell of a housing
Probably literally a 50 year old single wide trailer.

62132705
That sort of work is 1000 miles from nowhere.
You're on horseback.
So essentially you work 24x7 in rough conditions.
Worse than the military.
For worse than military pay.

62132714
>>62132619
This is why anti-immigration fags are so retarded. They think $3k a month is a pittance and think ranch hands deserve to make $150k.

Americans don't want to do these jobs you fucking retard.

62132585
Still more real than crypto

62132686
>>62132552

>666
>67
satan is real and he is gen z

item.title: How much BTC you holding right now? — 89 replies — 152.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much BTC you holding right now?

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-04-19 19:45 UTC

OP

How much BTC do you currently have in your wallet, and what’s your target amount to hold before it reaches $1M?

>I mean, if you don’t even have at least 1 BTC and you’re in crypto, it still feels pointless.

Early replies (first 3)

62114988
>>62114930
1.03

ill keep buying as pay cheques come in as long as we stay around the 4 year SMA

ill stop buying when we start running up and instead start selling to buy other assets, probably pay off my mortgage

62115186
>>62114930
i cope with my 5 stack and tell myself in 10 years i'll make it.

62115188
>>62114930
350 BTC or so.

Feels good. I'm living pretty modestly in a $2MM 5000 square foot house in a low cost state. Probably going to build a home gym extension with a sauna and hyperbaric oxygen chamber. Also a small fallout shelter just to be comfy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62123812
>>62115489
I'll retire early with that money

62123814
>>62123784
Why do you like risking when you can hold only

62123856
>>62115773
Not enough

62123911
>>62121496
Just sell it today bobo

62124101
>>62114930
I hold 0.84 btc at Kraken

62124449
>>62123590
You have a point here tho. The use cases are still early, so skepticism makes sense. What’s interesting is the idea of extending utility while keeping Bitcoin unchanged and kept in self custody.

62124459
>>62116911
>ETH is cool but why LINK?

62124487
2.3 BTC and 36 ETH.

62124590
>>62115225
Giga Chad, I kneel

62124643
>>62117007
His choice lol

62125521
>>62117185
$30k? Let's see if you're right.

62125560
1.4 BTC

It doesn't feel like enough. I'm 36 now, and my dream of living a wealthy and comfy life before the age of 40 is basically done for. Is it even possible to have a fun life at 50+? I really lament not getting into BTC back in 2014. It's not like I didn't know about it back then.

62126507
Is BTC even worth having anymore? Seems to have had pretty shit returns since the covid boom.

62126533
>>62114930
https://timesofmalta.com/article/elusive-bitcoin-founder-adam-back-lives-malta.1126648
What are the financial implications of this?

62126561
>>62123814
What risk are you talking about here? Also, it’s better now that there are returns instead of just holding with no yield. This setup is way better overall.

62126610
>>62117395
but why did you sell?

62126617
>>62117832
buy the bottom? but at which price?

62127919
>>62124487
More!!!

62128353
>>62120218
YOU CAN DO IT BOI

62128654
>>62125560
Don't worry about it. Keep going and have fun. YOLO.

62130077
>>62122141
Why FUD

62130099
0.3

62131443
>>62130099
Just 0.3? Make it whole bitcoin

62131447
>>62126507
since the COVID boom? It was just $8k during covid

62131531
>>62114930
The new paradigm is no more peaks.

62131569
>>62114930
Zero and zero

62132043
1.72 bitcoins
*sigh*
I keep stacking, maybe one day, I'll have 2 whole bitcoins.

62132222
around 10, i might end up keeping them forever as a backup plan. Looking at my taxes my average accumulation price was 29k and sell price was 109k Tidy little 70k each. I'll buy back in when the dump is over and it settles between 20-30k. for supposedly being a crypto board you all sure seem to struggle with basic pattern recognition, this is exactly what happens every time, dump 75% from the peak and then the next bull run starts.

62132243
>>62115773
I remember that 21 used to be the suistack. Everyone's goal was to accumulate AT LEAST 21 btc.

62132565
>>62131531
Why?

item.title: George LOSES it — 17 replies — 44.5/hr

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-04-19 19:57 UTC

OP

George goes BALLISTIC at one of his fans for calling him out for saying we were going to be in a supercycle, calls his fan "stupid" for trusting him, and says anybody who is stupid enough to trust him deserves to lose their money

30:10
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aoTlUw-Fy2M

Early replies (first 3)

62131622
>>62131613
>watching a crypto youtuber in 2026
Why do you morons watch these people? I haven't watched a crypto youtuber since 2017. At least back then they were somewhat relevant.

62131627
>carrot on a stick '''investment'''
lmao

62131685
>>62131613
>calls his fan "stupid" for trusting him, and says anybody who is stupid enough to trust him deserves to lose their money
he's not wrong

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62131693
who?

62131753
>>62131613
I hate Americans. Muh BLAME YOURSELF. When he clearly gave that retarded advice.

62131854
>>62131693
Jackie Chan's son is a Bitcoin influencer.

62132055
Hello

62132093
gongoup guy is not bullish? bottom signal

62132096
>>62132093
kek you are so rekt bro is unreal

62132376
>>62131685
hes not wrong, but its still a self dunk and disrespectful to people that bother to listen to him

people in the chat were typing "lol", and acting like he was epically owning that person. Makes one wonder of the average IQ of his fans. He basically said anybody who listens to his shit opinion seriously is a retard and his whole chat clapped

62132441
>>62132376
seriously? You literally can just look at a picture of this guy and know he is drooling fucking retard. Of course anyone that still listens to him is a drooling retard. Why do you care?

62132469
>>62132441
He's the first youtuber that I came to when I began crypto. I've been following him since 2022, and I've grew fond of him over the years.These last 5 months have been demystifying to me.

62132522
>>62132469
> last 5 months have been demystifying
took you long enough lol

62132538
>>62131613
Dear Mr. Gongo,
I've been your fan for a very long time. I watch your videos every day, but lately, you've disappointed me.

Signed,
an Anonymous

62132566
I admire his confidence, unironically. Someone so ugly, so retarded and so unlikeable and he sitll believes that what he says has so much value that he created 2 more channels with his same videos but translated with AI into Chinese and Spanish.

62132588
>>62132566
thats the indomitable human spirit

62132620
>>62132376
His audience is boomers buying on centralized exchanges who don't know what a blockchain is.

item.title: You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon? — 47 replies — 43.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon?

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-04-19 19:16 UTC

OP

You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62125227
>>62124756
they will get turned away, even if the crowd lined up at the current levels! there will only be 21 million coins and many people have more than one already. the crowds will be turned away since there is not enough of them left. i read that there are already something like 200k bitcoin holders and only a million coins left to be mined. even if each of those new coins went to a new holder (they won't) the maximum number of people that can have a bitcoin at the same time is 1.2 million.

62125283
I placed an order last weekend and initiated an ACH transfer to buy more tomorrow.
t. lucky retard

62125298
>>62125283
>lucky retard
well, half of that statement is true.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62126217
>>62124756
I picked xrp because $1 -> $1 million is a million x whereas $100k -> $1 million is only 10x

62126224
>had my buy orders at $63k
>dip barely clears $65k
Fuck I'm pretty sure I didn't miss the boat but this price action is demoralizing.

62126314
Why people would rush to buy Bitcoin at 1 mi when they didn't at 100k?

62126338
>>62126314
kek

if we even reach 200K next bullrun consider yourself very lucky

62126414
MY STRATEGIC BITCOIN RESERVE NOW HOLDS 0.22997 BTC!!!!!!

62126672
>>62126314
Protip, people are fucking retarded.

62126806
>>62126111
They can own Bitcoin, they may not own A bitcoin. It's only relevant if you're a math retard.

62126809
>>62126672
>>62126314
Why did people rush to buy Bitcoin at $10k when they didn't at $100?

62126824
i will never understand contemporary bitcoin shit
there are only 21,000,000 bitcoins in existence right?
if bitcoin hit $1,000,000 that would mean the bitcoin market cap would be $21 trillion
compare that to market caps of other industries rn, i don't see how it's physically possible

it seems like people desperately want to believe they can just fucking do what people did with bitcoin in the early 2010s and make a ridiculous amount of money just by uhhh... buying it again and waiting for it to happen again
stupid though

62126828
correct me if i'm wrong about any of that, i want to understand

62126833
is it all a joke?

62126984
>>62125310
I'm thinking of selling but I moht wait a little longer. This pump certainly won't last long though, so getting the timing right will be tough.

62126986
>>62125582
Even if all of this was truth, who cares? It can still make me money.

62126990
>>62126986
wtf is that supposed to mean
i mean, anything CAN make you money

62127238
>>62126824
Idk man it might still hit 300k at least which will hopefully gigapump my alts.

62127252
>>62127238
fair enough i guess

62127263
>>62126314
at $1m they will need it

62127315
>>62124756

62127745
>>62126414
well done, anon

62127808
>>62126824
You’re almost there anon. You’re on the precipice of figuring it out. Bitcoin will be the number one store of value in the world. Buy it before that happens.

62127809
>>62126809
it wasnt easy to buy at 100

62127812
>>62126824
/thread. new buyers are effn late.

62127822
>>62127812
If it’s lower than the MC of gold, which is an inferior store of value in every way, then you’re actually early.

62130754
>>62126824
>i will never understand contemporary bitcoin shit
>there are only 21,000,000 bitcoins in existence right?
the bitcoin is valuable because 21 argument is so low iq
and there's less than 21m in existence because of data loss

62131388
>>62124756
BTC already mooned long time ago, I bought for 300 USD/BTC and I was late to the party.

62131621
>>62124756
>goycattle going to the slaughter
pottery

62131649
>>62124756
i would waste my time explaining why believing bitcoin can reach 1 million is retarded but you either genuinely wouldn't understand what i'm saying because you actually are that stupid or you simply would put your hands on your ears and go "la la la la i can't hear you fud fud you are fudding have fun staying poor!!!!!!"
either way its a waste of my time so i'll just call you a retard.

retard.

62131666
>>62124756
>moon bag
it's going below 40k now that retards are saying shit like this

62132418
>>62124756
>moon bag
Hallo reddit

62132454
>People are still buying and believing in BTC after this massively disappointing limp dicked "bullrun" that just happened

item.title: NUCLEAR HAPPENING — 18 replies — 32.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
NUCLEAR HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-04-19 19:47 UTC

OP

CLARITY ACT HAS BEEN POSTPONED FOR AUGUST

Early replies (first 3)

62130929
>>62130927
lol

62130937
the who what?

so is SPY not gonna keep going up 1% per day this week?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62130941
>>62130935

62130945
>>62130927
oh linkies

62130946
>>62130927
nah fake news.

62130950
>>62130935
>>62130941

62130954
tf is the clarity act

i'm looking at the SPY chart trying to make sense of it
looks like it started to drop around feb 25th when the iran shit happened, but this spike since like april 6th, wtf is it
seems artificial to me

even if/when things SHOULD go down i'm wondering what sort of tricks the fed might have to keep it going up a little bit longer & sike me out

62130955
>>62130945
ain't dat dat one shitcoin?
does the clarity act relate to crypto reserves or something?

62130971
>>62130927
Really? Or are you shit posting?

62130980
>>62130971
it's unclear by the looks of it

62131072
>>62130927
They have literally started multiple wars to suppress LINK, delaying Clarity Act is the least they could do

62131163
>>62131072

62131235
>>62130927
No source, suck a turd op

62132463
>>62131072
The end result of these wars is to usher in the digital economy.

62132477
>>62131072
Why are they trying so hard to keep LINK below $9? What's so special about $9?

62132517
>>62130927
I still dont know what this act is and i dont care

62132572
>>62132477
3^2 is 9
3*2 is 6
6+6+6 is 18
1+8 is 9

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 73 replies — 15.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-04-19 15:35 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62121769
>>62121723
> every company goes under
Not quite true. Sure majority do go under, but successful companies adapt to economic changes, merge, or subsidize
Amazon created quite the monopoly in the retail sector. Hard to envision a downturn anytime soon.

>AWS pivots to Web3 cloud
Point taken. There again will DOT have a successful launch once all its compon8 are built in? Will it have enough marketing? Will a new challenger approach?

62121787
>>62121749
Most people would agree Linux is the superior OS against Windows OS

Who still owns the OS space?

Exactly

62121817
>>62121787
>Most people would agree Linux is the superior OS against Windows OS
Great that you point that out. That's an apples to oranges comparison. Linux isn't meant for the average user. It's meant for programmers in a lot of ways. We're also not talking about OS. Are we?

>>62121769
>Not quite true. Sure majority do go under, but successful companies adapt to economic changes, merge, or subsidize
True, and no company survives in it's original form. Everyone adapts to the time. And the time is shifting towards a Web3 cloud.

>Hard to envision a downturn anytime soon.
True, and I'm not saying that. What I'm saying is that Web3 will, over time, take a large share away from it, due to it being cheaper and having more uptime. Again, centralized servers CAN NOT compete with decentralized ones on uptime and cost. And those two factors are literally the only two important factors when it comes to a cloud provider. It has to be cheap and have the largest possible uptime.

>Point taken. There again will DOT have a successful launch once all its compon8 are built in? Will it have enough marketing? Will a new challenger approach?
The thing is that Amazon will just use Web3 clouds. I doubt they'll build their own because they'd have to spent a lot of money to re-invent the wheel. DOT is already ready in a lot of ways. This year JAM hits and that'll transform it into a TRUE Web3 cloud capable of handling the load of AWS. Look, I'm not saying that DOT will IMMEDIATELY kill everyone. It'll be a slow transition and more and more companies will be switching. You know the 20/80 rule? It takes as much time to go from 0% to 20% than it does from 20% to 80%. This was true for smart phones, the internet, cars, ... So, if it took 10 years to go from 0% users to 20% of the population using something then it'll take that same amount of time to go from 20% to 80%. TO BE CONTINUED

62121830
>>62121769
Copied from Google AI

As of early 2026, over 650 million to nearly 1 billion people worldwide are estimated to own or use cryptocurrency. Growth is driven by high adoption in regions like Asia and Africa, with about 6.8% of the global population holding digital assets. While ownership is high, active monthly users are estimated lower, around 30–60 million, indicating a large population of passive holders.

Projected Growth: The number of users is expected to reach 993 million by 2026, with user penetration expected to reach 12.63% of the population, according to 1.
Regional Leaders: Asia dominates with 43% of global users, followed by North America (17%) and Europe (15%).
Top Adoption Countries: India, Nigeria, and Indonesia show high adoption rates, often driven by local currency instability, according to 4 and 3.
User Demographics: Approximately 60% of crypto users are under the age of 34, with a majority being male.

Active on-chain usage continues to rise as mobile wallet installations grow, with over 1.1 billion installations tracked as of early 2026.


We're almost at the 20% mark. BTC was created 17 years ago. So, we'll be at the 20% mark by like 2028 to 2030. So like 19 to 21 years. So, by the year 2050 80% of the world population will be using Web3.

62121862
I'm not sure if you guys are looking at the chart but we're really close to breaking out from the bottom
Look at picrel >>62118020 and >>62118038

62121888
>>62121769
Will it have enough marketing?
No worries my brother. I have made a marketing contract with Jeny Smith (https://thothub.mx/videos/1505023/jeny-smith-polka-dot-pantyhose/). She will market DOT for us. We are set my Mumbai brother

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62121922
>>62121862
I'm not sure if you guys noticed. It's an adam and eve pattern on the 4h chart. Look at picrel

62121953
>>62121862
>>62121922

62122759
>>62121922
>>62121953

Chart looks good

But in the last thread weren't you shorting DOT

62123242
>>62122759
Panjali, be quite you fool (https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/61932834#p62069282). Do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat with their hands again? Or do you want to upgrade from our metal spoons to silver and then golden spoons? We, the Mumbai warriors, are painting the graph bullish in order to lure people in, then we strike with a x125 short. We actually been holding short open since 55$. The whole of Mumbai will eat from that singular short. Panjali, you have much to learn about the Vishnu ways. It's ok my son. I forgive you. Together we are strong, divided we are weak.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62124594
>>62123242
Way to promote a project then rug people

62125019
Didint read thread, i Will let My id decide if i buy some DOT when i get home

62125037
>>62125019
w4, E3, Wasb, and purple colour... Yes.. i see...

Not buying.

62125463
this is the most demoralizing dot thread

62126538
>>62124594
>>62125019
>>62125037
>>62125463
Good job my fellow warriors of Mumbai. Thank you for keeping the thread alive with your comments.

>this is the most demoralizing dot thread
How so my Mumbai brother?

>Way to promote a project then rug people
Yes Ganesha. We play the peoples hearts and then we stab them in their hearts.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62126677
Sol 8>297 Is 37X
Dot 1,15 x37= 42

62126931
>>62126538
Holy shit. Are you actually an autistic retard?

62126975
>>62126931
Kamal, are you getting upset again because your mother took away your metal spoon and ate with it? There is plenty of metal spoons for everyone my young warrior of Mumbai.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127244
>>62126677
Vrishank, that is really good mathematics. Education is really paying off I see. Very good. You might even become a doctor or a lawyer. Yes, you shall reach to the heavens of nirvana one day my young Vrishank. I, Vishnu Kumar, believe in you and so should you my young Vishnu warrior of the mind chain, my young Mumbai warrior, my young metal spoon owner. Soon, we will have silver spoons for everyone, then gold spoon and after that we reach the nirvana of diamond spoons. The whole of Mumbai will eat.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127578
>>62126975
Yep. You are officially a retard.

62127673
>>62127578
Vrishank, did you just really say that with the intent you did? Is there anything wrong? Are you not feeling ok?

62128205
>>62126931
>>62127578
you are talking to a bot (and so am I)

62128210
>>62128205
BIP BOP BIP BOP


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62129040
>>62128205
No, its actual autistic retard

62129456
>>62129040
Impossible, look at the time stamps of the posts

62129518
>>62129040
>>62129456
No, it's actually Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131415
100$ end of bull run


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131658
It is still cheap right now. Don't buy my bags at 25$. You can buy them now at 1.28$. Don't FOMO in at 50$ guys.

62131731
>>62116681
>What exactly does it have that sets it apart from Solona and ETH?
Solana has a success rate associated with it's transactions (see https://solscan.io/analytics). That's kind of fucked up that there's a 10 to 20% chance your tx might not get processed, right? I've never seen a DOT tx fail. DOT doesn't even measure failed tx https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily&relay-chain=polkadot&chains= because there are none. If you submit a tx it'll complete within like 6s to 30s. The longest a tx takes is when you transfer from one corechain to another. So when you bridge (use a HRMP channel) between corechains. And even that never took longer than like 40s from my personal experience. It's bizarre to me that literally up to 20% of the tx simply fail.
Another thing is that SOL literally has more "vote" transactions than anything else; https://solscan.io/analytics#txs . And vote transactions are LITERALLY validator messages to keep the network running. DOT doesn't count that as a tx at all. 60 to 80% of the tx on SOL are from validators to keep the network running. That's insane to think about that you need more tx to run the network than the users use to execute stuff. People are stupid for thinking that SOL processes 3k TPS. More than half of that is just to keep the network running. It's a literal scam number to be honest. It's just to make the normies believe that the network isn't a scam. The number of transactions is completely fake in a sense.
And again, the funny thing is that 1 core of DOT will be able to handle more TPS than the whole of SOL once either JAM or NOMT hit. Maybe even before that once the 500ms Basti blocks hit. If you combine 500ms blocks + JAM + NOMT one core of DOT will be able to probably do anywhere between 2 to 5x more than SOL as a whole and DOT has LITERALLY 100 cores and this will also probably be scaled to more sooner or later. It'll sooner or later scale to about 300 to 500 cores.

62131777
>>62116681
>>62131731
The 300-500 cores are after the network upgrades to 1000 validators from 600 and after a few more future optimizations. But DOT can then scale further by adding more relay chains. So, currently there is 1 relay chain that connects all the corechains (while they also have direct connections through the HRMP channels) and this relay chain has 600 active validators at any given time. At some point DOT could scale further by having multiple relay chains and separate validators on each relay chain and then having a "relay chain" that connects the relay chains. Sure, this would fragment liquidity in a sense but you could still transfer assets between the relay chains in probably like 30s or so... So in a sense the fragmented liquidity would be a non-problem. DOT is truly scalable while other chains simply are not. DOT covered all the problems because Gavin used to develop ETH and noticed "OMG, this won't work out" and then he left ETH in order to develop DOT. DOT is ETH done right in a sense. ETH is just BTC with smart contracts and DOT is an actual solution to all the problems that plague ETH and all the other smart contract platforms.

item.title: Lied my ass off, got a six figure job plz help. — 32 replies — 13.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Lied my ass off, got a six figure job plz help.

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-04-19 18:01 UTC

OP

okay anons, I'll be blunt as fuck.

Flat-out lied on my resume. Econ degree, data analyst gig at some regional firm. Only good thing was tons of free time so I grinded leetcode non-stop cuz I had nothing better to do.

made a fake resume, somehow snagged six-figure mid-level SWE job. 20 days pto, free food, 4 weeks work from anywhere.

Start date 4 weeks away. Can I just use Claude or Copilot to carry me? I have never done any shipping of actual code ever. never used terminal once. never touched real IDE. Never done code reviews. i just grinded python and leetcode 24/7.

please respond I'm scared as fuck. Am I cooked or does this actually work? Anything I should learn before even starting or should I just give up and not show up? Anyone pull this off?

Should i aim for product management instead?

Early replies (first 3)

62130795
remain confident and hedge your bets by applying elsewhere. do not think of yourself as in trouble, you are just a LARPing spy seeing how far you can make it to infiltrate their systems. people perform better when they think like this. just try to fit in, figure things out but be ready to jump ship when it's time. rinse and repeat and eventually you're legit

62130812
>>62130783
good job anon
stop panicking moron, worst case scenario is you DON'T get $100,000 woOOoOoo spooky
>please respond I'm scared as fuck. Am I cooked or does this actually work? Anything I should learn before even starting or should I just give up and not show up? Anyone pull this off?
nigger go to bed
stop ruminating
the less you overthink, the better chances you have, trust me

62130816
>>62130783
>Can I just use Claude or Copilot to carry me?
don't do this
>I have never done any shipping of actual code ever. never used terminal once. never touched real IDE. Never done code reviews. i just grinded python and leetcode 24/7.
just wing it
if you've done leetcode & had exposure to that shit you can do the rest, they're not gonna be doing anything high pressure
stay in the present moment & pay attention to what they're doing, don't get up in your own head about this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62130919
Invest in some online tutor to give you a crash course in some shit maybe

62130921
>>62130812
bump

chill out dude, you'll be fine literally just use chatgpt until you figure it out

62130924
>>62130783
Fuck tell me how you did this, I actually have projects and a degree and an aws cert, what did you add

62130940
>>62130783
Mythos might even be out in 4 weeks. Even if it's not AI can carry you for awhile. Especially if it's a larger company, it takes awhile before they can really say you're incompetent and fire you. what could kill you is getting into a face to face debate over something technical. so even if someone is clearly wrong just keep your mouth shut.

62130990
>>62130783
We dont help indians on this board, saar.

62131088
>>62130783
your best bet is to ask your colleagues for help. but only do it sparingly and write down what they tell you and internalize it. for a data analyst role, you have to learn sql, possibly git

62131267
>>62130783
Bro I've been in IT and project delivery for 15 years and landed a startup job and I feel like im fucked but i think everyone at this stage is just larping anyway people just talk and talk "clean code, beautiful code, were agile" its boring its like people think coding is some art form and hold themselves so highly but really they are just wagies like everyone else and nobody cares as long as functionally its right the customer dont actually care what the code looks like

And retros are just group therapy with one guy swinging his dick like hes God but is really just a narcissist and scared to fail.

62131608
>>62130783
Based bro Indiamaxxing

62131675
>>62130783
>data analysis
Literally just use shitty AIs and mass horde as much of your income as possible and prepare to be caught.

62131683
>>62130783
Congratulations, 4 weeks is more than enough time to fake it better

62131688
>>62130783
Good news are you if you got in, the inertia will keep you for half year at least. If you keep proactively learning, people will notice you're fake but let it slide because you're providing value.

I had serious struggle rooting parasites like you, 5000-head company.

62131696
>>62130795
this. Op could have a 30 year career with nothing but confidence. Many such cases. That's why things are fucked, the boomers in charge never knew what the fuck they were doing either.

>your reckoning might never come you retarded piece of shit

62131714
>>62130783

I had a dream like this where I landed some big job that I wasn't qualified for, then when I show up to work they take me in front of a fucking nuclear reactor and tell me to fix it and when I can't they have security escort me out while everyone watches.

So good luck and I hope you do better!

62131724
>>62130783
>mid-level SWE job
>>62130783
>never used terminal once. never touched real IDE. Never done code reviews.

ITfag here (cybersecurity). It you were in cybersecurity you could get away with this and learn git and how to use github and VS Studio Code and shit and just lean on your other expertise while you use ChatGPT to learn the CI/CD pipeline and tools.

But as a MID LEVEL SOFTWARE ENGINEER you are completely fucked and there is no way you are gonna pull this off but put me in the screencap when you come back and tell us how you embarrassed yourself and your chink/gook/jeet coworkers reported you to Chad Kinsington your team lead and he had HR Stacy fire you.

62131729
>>62130783
Troon out immediatly. Update your goybook and instacuck status and upload photos of you trooning out and dancing in gay bars. They can't fire you unless they do it because of transphobic reasons. They'll offer you severance for 2 years.

62131767
>>62130783
Don’t get emotional about your work

62131802
>>62130783
>Flat-out lied on my resume
>made a fake resume, somehow snagged six-figure mid-level SWE job
Congrats on becoming the typical H1B jeet. Now you just keep jeeting the company hy faking confidence and googling all the answers.
>Am I cooked or does this actually work?
Look around your office and see how many jeets you have. None of them do real work. Everyone of them learned just enough to scrape by. You're one of them now, the jeet class.
No shame in that when this is a market where you need to jeet or get jeeted.

62131813
>>62130783
based if you're white but either way who cares? what are they gonna do, fire you?
that takes a while.
Then you apply to the next job and use a burner cell to give yourself a good reference

62131815
>>62130783
The entire industry is just a bunch of lies, nothing's based on merit. Just kill yourself, bitch.

62131820
>>62130924
Are you really this helpless? You deserve to be unemployed.
>how to?
>find defunct company in [industry]
>put defuct company on your resume as [title of interest] with xyz years of experience
>"yes I was the techlead for the backend development at Toys R Us, Radioshack, and Blockbuster before they went out of business"
Use your brain anon. No one can verify your experience if the big name company no longer exists. Look for recent bankruptcies in your industry.

>>62131696
>Op could have a 30 year career with nothing but confidence
/thread
All jobs are fake anyways.

62131826
>>62130783
Sounds fake because they didnt check your degree.

62131836
>>62130783
>Flat-out lied on my resume
good. thats what EVERYONE does. its part of the game. rest is up to you. fake it until you make it.

62131845
>>62131802
>describes my resume
damn AI is getting good these days

62131848
>>62130783
Most jobs are actually pretty easy

62131852
>>62130783
Just wing it, ask your juniors how you do things in this company. Just be curious, keep learning and keep grinding. First 3 months will be the hardest. You can practice for free contributing to open-source software.

62132120
>>62130783
Keep faking it until you make it. But don't just sit there, use this opportunity to learn.

At worst they'll see right through you and let you go before probation but you make some good money anyways and learn a few things and get humbled. But you'll see how the business is organized how they operation, what tools they use, etc. this is valuable knowledge on its own, even if its just for personal use.

At best you find out the job is easier than it seems and that you can quickly get the ropes and start learning on the job,
BUT put the effort to learn.
You will get humbled, and you'll probably fuck up, but if you make it past probation, then its the companies fault for hiring you in the first place. Just don't do anything stupid and take responsibility for anything critical. This is an Opportunity. Seize it.

62132148
being a nice person with a positive attitude is arguably more important than technical competence. technical problems generally are solved and you can find the solution either thinking about it or with AI. not being a jerk is much harder.

62132167
>>62132148
This post encapsulates every thing I wanted to say. Social skills are the best thing anyone can have in life. I work as a programmer and I’ve completely checked out of my job, I mostly use Gemini to do data science, I give it the first 10 lines of each file and tell it to write the code exactly as I need it. The less prompting you do, the better the code quality. If you take too many prompts AI will start forgetting things and will leave out critical functions.

62132188
>>62130783
just stack cash, appear productive and do the bare minimum to get by
simple as

item.title: Financial Advice for a Zoomerfag — 10 replies — 13.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Financial Advice for a Zoomerfag

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-04-19 19:36 UTC

OP

I'm tired of looking at 40% dry powder. collectibles are pokemon cards, crypto is all BTC, Equities is all S&P. Where do I blow this cash. Biztard to Biztard. No debt, live with parents, no real expenses. Holding this little BTC makes me feel retarded. I'm a 20 year old zoomer and these assets feel too big to matter to me. I feel stuck, and might just pivot to something else completely. Idk... Just trying to figure this all out.

I'm not dumb for thinking it's pointless to DCA into these massive mcaps right? Being this young seems dumb to feed zog especially.

Early replies (first 3)

62132324
>>62132289
>Holding this little BTC makes me feel retarded
Comparatively, it's weird that holding that amount in the S&P doesn't make you feel retarded. The 7% avg return won't mean shit with 5k.
>10k cash
I don't know what your monthly expenses are, but I think you're good halving that

62132331
>>62132289
put the rest into stocks and dont look at it for 190 years

62132395
>>62132289
I know you won't take this advice, but read mark minervini and William O'Neal's books to learn how to invest. Minervini won the us investing championship with 300% gains and has read thousands of investing books

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62132422
>>62132324
>The 7% avg return
it is not 7%
go look at the last 10 years
but also as a youngfag you should take more risk really

62132424
you are a retard and a fag. Spend your energy increasing your job earnings and quitting being a low rent loser with only 20k and faggy pokemon biceps.

90% VOO
8% IBIT
2% Cash IF AND ONLY IF you are going to us it to buy dips when trump tweets something or whatever.
you're welcome.

62132428
21k worth of assets isn't anything to give much thought about diversifying and percentages.
Keep an emergency fund cash and the rest in index funds and forget about it.
Live your life, increase your income, add to your pot when you can.

62132439
>>62132289
>Where do I blow this cash
ladyboys in thailand

62132516
>>62132395
ill have gemini summarize the books kek

62132518
>>62132422
>go look at the last 10 years
I could likewise say 'go look at 2000-2010' or something, a decade is fucking nothing

62132521
>>62132324
monthly expenses are just phone bill, spotify and gas. which is $150/month. I have rent, car insurance, food fully covered. im lucky.

item.title: >tfw parents have no saving >or 401k >or investments so um what the FUCK am i supposed to do when they get old and cant… — 18 replies — 12.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
>tfw parents have no saving >or 401k >or investments so um what the FUCK am i supposed to do when they get old and cant…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-04-19 18:51 UTC

OP

>tfw parents have no saving
>or 401k
>or investments

so um what the FUCK am i supposed to do when they get old and cant work lmao. I'm going to have to take care of my own family and kids + myself + their care and life + saving for my own retirement???? HOW???????

Early replies (first 3)

62130654
just close your eyes and pretend they don't exist

62130656
>>62130650
If they couldn't do it during the easiest time, it will be less likely that you will do it.

62130657
>>62130650
the gov will give them rental assistance for a shitty apartment in a bad area of town. they will be poor and live off social security.

this happened to a divorced guy i knew. she took his house and half his 401k. then he lost his job at 60 and couldnt get another one. he "died" a few years later.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62130684
>>62130650
this >>62130654

or work harder/acquire more wealth

in the past, peoples parents usually came to live with a family member once they got too old
if you put them in a nursing home they'll get sedated and beaten up by black people and watch TV so keep that inmind

it's actually not that hard/expensive to take care of them, just let them live with you & feed them
u don't have to pay the medical jew and probably shouldn't

62130687
>>62130657
>this happened to a divorced guy i knew. she took his house and half his 401k. then he lost his job at 60 and couldnt get another one. he "died" a few years later.
RIP

i hate the antichrist

62130691
they'll probably go on medicaid and/or social security disability. low-end assisted living, free clinics, what this guy said >>62130657
naturally they'll depend on you more to get by month-to-month. no fancy gated community or premium caretakers

62130695
Get on social security and move to central/south america

62130696
>>62130687
yeah he was really a nice guy. he lived next-door to us growing up. good luck to everyone bros

62130697
>>62130650
pull yourself up by the bootstraps sonny boy. a real man like myself worked a part time job to buy a Miami beach house for my parents to retire comfortably in.

62130722
dunno. live in a trailer park. that's cheap as fuck.

62130729
>>62130696
>good luck to everyone bros
likewise anon
>>62130722
honestly might not be a bad idea
i have cousins who live in a mobile home area outside a city in texas, they're poor but it's comfy as hell, no problems with neighbors etc

62130730
>>62130650
My dad died and I lost money because I had to pay about 5k for funeral costs and to have his apartment cleaned. He had no savings, was 66

62130750
With about 250k rough net worth, including retirement money by 30, you likely won't even need inheritance to survive.
Whats youre excuse?

62130761
>>62130750
>youre

62130772
>>62130761
Thsi inst docisrd, fgagot. I cna splel hevower i wnat.

62130834
>>62130772

62130859
>>62130650
I’m not taking care of my parents. They either planned accordingly or they’re dying in the street

62132342
>>62130684
>let them live with you
that sucks

item.title: >setup TailOS >make a monero wallet >register on dr*gh*b >setup and link pgp key >try to directly buy XMR >the only webs… — 8 replies — 8.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>setup TailOS >make a monero wallet >register on dr*gh*b >setup and link pgp key >try to directly buy XMR >the only webs…

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-04-19 19:22 UTC

OP

>setup TailOS
>make a monero wallet
>register on dr*gh*b
>setup and link pgp key
>try to directly buy XMR
>the only websites that let you buy XMR have ID verification and 3-7 day holds of funds
>fuck it, try to buy bitcoin and transfer to XMR
>"We're sorry, we don't support Amerikkka"
>"This purchase is unlikely to go through with this card(master card)"
>look at all my cards
>they're all mastercard
>search for a simple website that just takes my fucking money, and sends me bitcoin
>it doesn't exist, if it does, it requires ID verification
Am I fucking retarded or something? How do they fuck this up, the intent behind crypto was anonymity, yet I CAN'T FUCKING USE IT WITHOUT ID VERIFICATION
Kraken already is holding my funds until they can leech the interest shekels out of them, but I need it TODAY

Early replies (first 3)

62131812
>AHHHHH IM TRAPPED IN THE TRANNYCOINVERSE SAVE ME NIGGERMAN

62131957
>>62131805
+5 years late bro
I have non-kyc funds and I had to start KYC wallets.
Perhaps my non-kyc crypto will be worth more one day, or illegal to use without an ID attached.
I don't know how else to do it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62132069
>>62131805
Never ever ever put cash from or too kraken. Use it for swapping and tx'ing only. Have to do this will all the casinos I use. Buy buttcoin on strike, transfer it to kraken, swap it to whatever coin im using at the time (99% tether), then send out. The payment center doesn't have to verify or kyc me because Kraken has already done it (I'm not some nigeria or pajeet trying to scam). Thats all its good for, its not even worth trading on anymore.

62132415
>>62131805
every faggot nigger government in existence all around the world and payment processor and bank forces these people to all require ID..

They have no choice this isnt 2015 anymore sadly.

62132446
>>62131812
>I'm Barack Obama, and I'm gay.

62132465
>>62131805
>not using an ime, camera, microphone, speaker, and wireless module neutered machine with coreboot in a fariday cage
ngmi

62132476
>>62131921
Yea, cause I can just go buy drugs with fiat money. Idiot.
>>62131957
I'm not investing bro, trying to get high
>>62132069
solid advice, i'll follow it from now on
>>62132415
makes no sense, need to jump through a bunch of hoops just to unlink you from the coins

item.title: >tech is oversaturated >law is oversaturated >medicine is oversaturated >trades are oversaturated >data science is overs… — 33 replies — 5.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
>tech is oversaturated >law is oversaturated >medicine is oversaturated >trades are oversaturated >data science is overs…

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-04-19 14:33 UTC

OP

>tech is oversaturated
>law is oversaturated
>medicine is oversaturated
>trades are oversaturated
>data science is oversaturated
>academia is oversaturated
>biology is oversaturated
>chemistry is oversaturated
>pharmacology is oversaturated
>IT is oversaturated
>finance is oversaturated
>cybersecurity is oversaturated
>design is oversaturated
>UI is oversaturated
>gaming is oversaturated
>marketing is oversaturated
>communications is oversaturated
>photography is oversaturated
>art is oversaturated
>music is oversaturated
>writing is oversaturated
>'content creation' is oversaturated
>onlyfans is oversaturated
>libraries are oversaturated
>criminal justice is oversaturated
>truck driving is oversaturated
>doordashing is oversaturated
>welfare is oversaturated
>the homeless shelter is oversaturated

Early replies (first 3)

62127924
rum ID

62127927
most of those things aren't oversaturated, they're gatekept for "job security" by dysgenic fucks who sit around with their dick/pussy in their hand at the expense of everyone else
art, design, cybersecurity, many others you listed

these jobs NEED to be done

62127929
it's just only retards are doing them
it's a strange state of affairs, but i think it's dumb/reductive to call everything oversaturated

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62128036
>>62127923
do engineering

62128051
>>62127923
>>trades are oversaturated
No, they aren't. It's just that there are too many illegal spics taking low wages to keep the job

62128055
>>62127924
Yarr

62128075
>>62127923
The warehouse isn't oversaturated, wagie

62128088
>>62127923
Healthcare is not oversaturated you retard. You just dont have the intestinal fortitude to go through the years of training requiring to get licensed and certified

62128096
>>62127923
med is the only true career everything else is shit

you will NEVER be out of a job as a doctor post residency, you could even start ur own private practice and u get paid insane money private and public.

62128164
When you join a fortnite server and there's more than 10 players in it are you like, man this is too oversaturated?

62128176
>>62128096
>We're not in end stage statism, my artificial "career" will surely be safe for the next 40 years
kek

62128708
>>62128096
Nord is the only true career.

62128710
>>62127923
>photography is oversaturated
Kek

62128764
>>62127923
It's because demand is low and that's because birthrates are cooked.

62128873
>>62127923
>medicine is oversaturated
America is so short doctors they're letting 3rd world browns come in and skip residency now. 100,000+ doctor shortage by 2035.

62128880
invest in the pods?

62128883
>>62127923
I have an open offer for cute girls/femboys to become my live in house maid and no one has applied, clearly the market isn't saturated.

62128888
>>62127923
I kind of want to start a huberman style podcast but less jew faggotry and more fringe stuff and grey market drugs.

62128895
>>62128036
Engineering sucks ass. You'll be surrounded by autistic ass kissing jeets and boomers. They'll all care way too much about some dumb shit that doesn't matter. It's hard to describe how demoralizing it is but I'm starting to wish I worked with normies instead.

62128896
>>62127923
Healthcare isn't oversaturated but it requires a bunch of training certifications, a lot of work to even get close to 100k, and it is both physically & mentally exhausting. You can not handle an ER job if you can't handle the possibility of seeing a baby with an impacted skull. Etc.

62128898
>>62128075
Keek, this

62128930
>>62127923
SARS-COVID-1 and -2 were supposed to solve this problem but they just weren't lethal enough.

62128970
>>62128075

62128974
>>62127924
Baaed

62128995
>>62128096
Surgeons and people in trauma wards and icus are the only real doctors, a lot of you are just rentboys for insurance and pharmaceutical agencies who are calling the shots

62129044
>>62128930
If you want to get rid of useless eaters the best solution is war not diseases.

But don't worry, every developed countries are arming themselves now so in the decades to come WW3 will start to kill those made irrelevant by AI.

62129110
>>62129044
UBI is all about socialism working this time. I'll probably bet against it happening, but just my opinion.

also, I think AI will fade as a fad in about 1-2 years just like the VR fad died
>hurr durr see you in the Metaverse
and then the retard boomers will be forced to start hiring again. they're in denial right now, thinking they could automate shit

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6p1Me03BPhM

62131215
>>62128880
Yes

62131342
>>62128036
oversaturated, there are a hundred engineering grads for every entry level engineering job on the market

62131346
>Starting your own business is oversaturated

62131355
Medicine is NOT oversaturated. T. A doctor

>>62128176
Funny that you mention 40 years because it’s projected that the amount of old people in western countries will increase for the next 40 years. Not all professions face the same circumstances.

62131391
>>62128088
>>62128096
>>62128896
>>62131355
Is this a coordinated shill campaign or is /biz/ full of doctors for some strange reason

62131616
Lawyer who needs to work on Sunday checking in.

item.title: Groundwork and research in starting a buisness — 9 replies — 4.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Groundwork and research in starting a buisness

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-04-19 18:26 UTC

OP

I'm a man with a STEM background who has worked in kitchens before to make it by but isn't trained in business, just project management. I have ideas for some brick and mortar businesses like novelty themed restaurants and hobby/game shops and I'd like to begin learning the fundamentals of starting and operating my own business, not sure if these would be LLCs or classified as something else. Didn't see (or could identify) a general for this kind of topic or question so I will ask the board, where do I go from here? Clearly anyone can start a business but I don't actually want to start making risks based around my ignorance, there will be plenty of room for risks on the financial side of things.
If there are resources or good guides, I'm interested. Obviously the business hook, product, and how I choose to market I won't want to copy brainlessly, I'm thinking more the actual startup, creation and admin side of things.
Pic unrelated.

Early replies (first 3)

62131465
>>62131451
You need to figure a lot of this out yourself otherwise its just going to flop anyway. Start with just registering/incorporating the company. LLC is best if you aren't a startup looking for vc funding or to sell out quickly.

If you're planning to do it alone/super lean team to start with then figure out what things are worth doing inhouse and what others you'll just be paying professional services for because you don't have time to do it all

62131614
>>62131451
/biz/ is a crypto board, take this pathetic wagie shit somewhere else.

>I have ideas for some brick and mortar businesses like novelty themed restaurants and hobby/game shops

These are all fucking terrible business ideas.

62131628
don't listen to the angry link marines, they are very upset right now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62131662
>>62131614
Spoken like a true incel. Kys.
>>62131451
Don’t reinvent the wheel. Just copy an existing working business (lead gen agency) and do it yourself. You need a proven offer with demand. Then just execute. Having LLC won’t give you anything, having paying customers wheel.

62131663
>>62131451
I finally started my own company about 3 years ago. It's not even remotely as difficult as other businesses make it seems, and it's easier than working for anyone else. If you don't have any experience specifically in what you want to start, do a little research, know your target audience, and find a good place to set up.

62131679
>>62131662
>>62131628
Chop chop wagies, only 20 hours until your work week starts.

62131726
>>62131451
Do a lot of research. Ask people where they like to go when they go out for fun. Ask where they've been in the past. Don't ask them hypotheticals, don't ask if they would go to X-themed restaurant or Y-hobby store. Most people won't respond fully honestly to those kinds of questions, they'll just say whatever sounds generally supportive. Don't even give them an idea of what you're planning, just focus on where they actually spend their money/time.
Remember that research is cheap/free, and the last thing you want to do is spend a fortune and months/years building some business that nobody wants.

62131738
>>62131451
posting in actual /biz/ thread. wtf are you doing here? people only sperg about random coins you haven't heard of here.

For food shit, the health code cert in addition to all the other usual business license + sellers' license seemed annoying and you aren't supposed to use (YOUR) kitchen, you're supposed to rent a commercial kitchen technically at least in Commiefornia. So you either operate illegally and wait to get shut down like the illegals do or you have massive capital investment up front, taking on huge initial risk like no normal person can afford to do. It's designed to be shitty in my humble opinion and I don't even know anything about food I just thought I could sell some iced coffee at flea markets where there were no beverages there wtf. complicated

62132265
>>62131738
You're right, I've had a food handler's card myself before but never those other certs, makes sense. I'll look more into food selling codes for my area. I'm sure taxation on restaurant income ontop of state tax will also need to be adhered to.
>>62131663
Best of luck on your business, anon. I know game stores, and the local university/military base is actually quite a distance away from another store that does video game and tcg tournaments, so for that store I'm hoping it will be my niche.
>>62131465
Thanks. I have a strong idea for the restaurant but would genuinely need to bring in talent from cooks and help building the menu, and I probably want a bar and to do alcohol, which is another cert on top of that.
